title: manihot herbacea
intro: Manihot herbacea, also known as the wild cassava or tapioca, is a perennial shrub native to South America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in health benefits, such as reducing swelling and pain, and has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Manihot herbacea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally, although it is essential to note that proper preparation and dosing are crucial to avoid adverse effects.
